question: Should people avoid statins because they cause dementia and muscle damage?
topic: Counters dangerous statin misinformation with evidence from 26+ randomized controlled trials showing

sources.name: FDA Drug Safety Communication: Important safety label changes to cholesterol-lowering statin drugs — FDA

discovery_sources.text: 26 randomized controlled trials demonstrate consistent cardiovascular benefit from cholesterol lowering—claim appears supported by current evidence
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Spring Break Rerun: Cholester-all In! The Role of Cholesterol in Heart Health

discovery_sources.text: Statins have no effect on dementia risk; may reduce dementia incidence through stroke prevention—should verify against latest neurology literature
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Spring Break Rerun: Cholester-all In! The Role of Cholesterol in Heart Health

discovery_sources.text: Statin-associated muscle pain incidence is very low and largely attributable to nocebo effect in placebo-controlled trials—supported by cited research
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Spring Break Rerun: Cholester-all In! The Role of Cholesterol in Heart Health

discovery_sources.text: LDL cholesterol can safely be lowered to near-zero levels without harm—should verify upper limits of safety data
